# Test Review — `lib/features/speech/` + `test/features/speech/` (root overview)

**Scope:** Root-level summary covering all child subdirectory reports for the speech feature.  
**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis).**

---

## Root-level test files

There are no test files directly in `test/features/speech/` (root). All tests are in subdirectories. This file serves as a navigation index.

---

## Cross-cutting issues (all subdirs)

1. **Centralized mocks not used.** `MockAudioRecorderRepository`, `MockPlayer`, `MockPlayerState`, `MockPlayerStream`, `FakePlayable` are defined inline in at least 5 test files (`recorder_controller_test`, `audio_player_controller_test`, `audio_checkbox_settings_test`, `audio_recording_modal_test`, `audio_recording_modal_coverage_test`). All of these already exist in `test/mocks/mocks.dart`. The inline copies should be deleted and imports added.

2. **Inline GetIt manipulation.** Multiple files use `if (getIt.isRegistered<X>()) { getIt.unregister<X>(); } getIt.registerSingleton<X>(...)` patterns instead of `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()` from `test/widget_test_utils.dart`.

3. **`pumpAndSettle` overuse.** 14 calls in `audio_recording_modal_coverage_test.dart`, 28 in `transcripts_list_item_test.dart`, 8+ in `analog_vu_meter_test.dart` (including 2 with `Duration(seconds: 2)`). Combined worst-case timeout risk: hundreds of CI seconds.

4. **Two test files for one source file.** `audio_recording_modal.dart` is tested by both `audio_recording_modal_test.dart` and `audio_recording_modal_coverage_test.dart`. Merge them.

5. **Missing test file.** `checkbox_visibility_provider.dart` (63 lines) has no test file. This is the highest-priority coverage gap in the feature.

---

## Top feature-wide opportunities (action checklist)

- [x] **[HIGH]** Merge `recording/audio_recording_modal_test.dart` + `recording/audio_recording_modal_coverage_test.dart` into one file (one-file-per-source) and replace the 14 `pumpAndSettle()` calls with bounded `tester.pump()`. **RESOLVED (stale):** already done in the recording-cluster pass — `audio_recording_modal_coverage_test.dart` no longer exists (merged into `audio_recording_modal_test.dart`) and the surviving file contains zero `pumpAndSettle` calls.
- [x] **[HIGH]** Create `test/features/speech/state/checkbox_visibility_provider_test.dart` covering `hasProfileTranscription` async branches and `checkboxVisibility` null/loading/error/data paths (only completely untested file in the feature). **RESOLVED (stale):** `test/features/speech/state/checkbox_visibility_provider_test.dart` exists and covers `hasProfileTranscription` (delegation, false path, stream-driven re-evaluation) and all four `checkboxVisibility` branches (null linkedId short-circuit, loading, error, data true/false). All 8 tests pass.
- [x] **[HIGH]** Split `state/recorder_controller_test.dart` (2327 lines) at the realtime/non-realtime boundary; mirror any split of `recorder_controller.dart` (734 lines). **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the test split is conditioned on a `recorder_controller.dart` split ("mirror any split"); as a standalone test split it would violate the one-test-file-per-source rule. The impl file (734 lines) is within limits, so neither half applies.
- [ ] **[MED]** Extract an error-handling helper in `state/audio_player_controller_test.dart` to eliminate the 5 × ~40-line duplicated container-setup blocks.
- [ ] **[MED]** Replace inline `MockAudioRecorderRepository`/`MockPlayer`/`MockPlayerState`/`FakePlayable` (5+ files) with the centralized mocks in `test/mocks/mocks.dart`, and inline GetIt wiring with `setUpTestGetIt()`/`tearDownTestGetIt()`.
- [ ] **[MED]** Cut `pumpAndSettle` overuse: `speech_modal/transcripts_list_item_test.dart` (×28), `recording/analog_vu_meter_test.dart` (×2 at `Duration(seconds: 2)`).
- [ ] **[LOW]** Add Glados property tests for `formatAudioDuration` (audio_progress_bar), `_calculateVu` (recorder_controller), and `AudioRecordingSignalLevel.fromDbfs` (audio_recording_orb) — pure functions with clamp/format invariants.

> Detailed, per-file action items live in the subdir reports linked above.
